# Codex DIFF gate — I-perm-024 (#1216) FOLLOW-UP: wire extended metrics into the LIVE scorer

HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter 1 of 5.
- Front-load ALL real findings in iter 1. No drip-feeding.
- Reserve P0/P1 for real execution risks; cosmetic = P3/P2.
- If iter 5 returns REQUEST_CHANGES, force-APPROVE on non-P0/P1; no iter 6.
- Verdict APPROVE iff zero NOVEL P0 AND zero continuing P0 AND zero P1.

## Output schema (required)
```yaml
verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES
novel_p0: [...]
continuing_p0: [...]
p1: [...]
p2: [...]
convergence_call: continue | accept_remaining
remaining_blockers_for_execution: [...]
```

## Why this follow-up exists (the operator's explicit concern)
A wiring forensic audit (`.codex/wiring_audit/verdict.txt`) found that the #1216 extended-metrics
module (`src/polaris_graph/benchmark/extended_metrics.py`, already committed in dd9f88f9 with
brief+diff APPROVE) was **INERT**: its only caller was `run_scorecard.py`, which is NOT invoked in any
live scoring path. The LIVE Path-B per-(system, question) scorer is
`scripts/dr_benchmark/score_run.py` (`score_one`), driven by the benchmark aggregator. This diff wires
the extended metrics into THAT live scorer so the five claim-by-claim metrics actually compute on a
real run.

## The diff (`.codex/I-perm-024/score_run_wiring.patch`, unstaged, +51 / one file)
EDIT `scripts/dr_benchmark/score_run.py`: added `import os`; in `score_one`, AFTER the existing `out`
dict is built and BEFORE the `polaris_gate_identity` append, a block gated by env
`PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS` (default OFF) computes `out["extended"]` from the already-built `rows`
(`list[ClaimRow]`) + `rubric_elements` (`list[RubricElement]`) + the pre-registered safety-floor ids.

## Evidence pack — I have ALSO checked these and they're clean (VERIFY, don't re-hunt)
1. **Signature match (no silent TypeError).** `extended_metrics.py` defines:
   - `faithfulness_precision(rows: list[ClaimRow])`, `citation_support_rate(rows: list[ClaimRow])`,
     `diversity_score(rows: list[ClaimRow])` — score_run passes `rows` (the `ClaimRow[]` built from
     `ledger.claims` at score_run.py:145-156). ✓
   - `required_entity_recall(rubric: list[RubricElement] | None)`,
     `safety_floor_recall(rubric, safety_element_ids)` — score_run passes `rubric_elements` (the
     `RubricElement[]` built at score_run.py:160-172) + `_safety_ids`. ✓
   - `load_safety_floor_element_ids(question_id) -> set[str]`. ✓
2. **`import os`** is present (score_run.py top, with argparse/json/sys) — no NameError.
3. **Default-OFF byte-identical.** The block runs only when `PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS` ∈
   {1,true,yes,on}. OFF → `out` is unchanged → scored JSON byte-identical. Confirm.
4. **§-1.1 structural guarantee.** Every metric function takes ONLY `ClaimRow` / `RubricElement` typed
   inputs (the module docstring's load-bearing property). score_run passes the AUDITED `rows` and
   `rubric_elements`, NEVER raw report text. So string-presence / pattern-presence is structurally
   impossible here. Confirm the wiring did not introduce any raw-text source.
5. **Dedup intentionally SKIPPED (`dedup_applied: False`) — verify this is sound, not an inflation
   hole.** The Claimify dedup path (`compute_extended_metrics` → `ScoredClaim(text, row)`) needs a
   per-claim `text` to cluster on. The reconciled-ledger `ClaimRow` carries NO `.text` field, and the
   reconciled ledger's claims are already atomic + unique by `claim_id` (the dual-§-1.1 audit
   deduplicates upstream). So there is no inflation vector to collapse here and no text to dedup on.
   The block calls the individual metric functions on `rows` directly. Confirm: (a) `ClaimRow` indeed
   has no text field; (b) skipping dedup cannot let a verbose system inflate `faithfulness_precision`
   / `citation_support_rate` (denominator = material atoms from the reconciled ledger, which is
   already deduplicated). A `methodology_note` in `out["extended"]` discloses the skip + the §-1.1
   provenance.

## §-1.1 / faithfulness — red-team this
- Confirm `out["extended"]` is purely additive (a new key) and does NOT alter `passed`, `reasons`,
  `lane1`, `lane2`, or any release/pass decision.
- Confirm none of the five values is used as a PASS/FAIL gate — they are diagnostics surfaced in the
  scored JSON, scored identically for polaris/chatgpt/gemini.
- `diversity_score` carries its DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY note (not a superiority signal). Confirm it is not
  framed as a "win".
- `safety_floor_recall` denominator = the PRE-REGISTERED tagged count, so a tagged id missing from the
  rubric counts AGAINST recall (fail-safe). Confirm the wiring preserves that (it passes the full
  rubric_elements + the registry ids).

## Honest scope
This is a pure WIRING follow-up: no metric logic changed (that was APPROVE'd in dd9f88f9). The only
new code is the call site in the live scorer + the default-OFF env gate + the slate force-on
(`PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS=1` in `run_gate_b.py`, already committed). Build: extended-metrics tests
(~30) pass; score_run imports + compiles.

 succeeded in 302276ms:
   1: """I-perm-024 (#1216) — beat-both scorer metric extension (claim-by-claim only).
   2: 
   3: Five extended metrics for the DR head-to-head, computed STRICTLY from the
   4: already-audited per-claim ledger (``ClaimRow``s — each a reconciled VERIFIED/
   5: PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE verdict against the FETCHED cited span)
   6: and the FROZEN gold rubric (``RubricElement``s whose ``covered`` /
   7: ``citation_supported`` flags were set by the SAME dual §-1.1 audit).
   8: 
   9: §-1.1 STRUCTURAL GUARANTEE (the load-bearing safety property): every function here
  10: accepts ONLY ``ClaimRow`` / ``RubricElement`` typed inputs. None of them receives or
  11: opens raw report text. So they CANNOT do string-presence / pattern-presence matching
  12: ("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?") — that is structurally impossible, not a
  13: convention. Each metric is a roll-up over the per-claim audit LEDGER, never over raw
  14: text. This is what keeps ``required_entity_recall`` / ``diversity_score`` off the
  15: §-1.1 BANNED list (word/citation/source COUNTS as quality, pattern-presence,
  16: string-presence PASS/FAIL — "lethal in clinical").
  17: 
  18: The metrics:
  19:   1. ``faithfulness_precision`` = VERIFIED / material(S0-S2).
  20:   2. ``citation_support_rate`` = VERIFIED-with-a-RESOLVED-citation / material.
  21:   3. ``required_entity_recall`` = covered+citation_supported frozen rubric elements
  22:      / total (the existing ``lane2_coverage`` semantics, surfaced as recall).
  23:   4. ``safety_floor_recall`` = #3 restricted to the PRE-REGISTERED safety element
  24:      subset (``safety_floor_elements_v3.json``, pinned to the rubric sha).
  25:   5. ``diversity_score`` = distinct resolved sources among VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED
  26:      count — DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (support-concentration), NOT a superiority signal.
  27: 
  28: Claimify-style dedup (``claim_dedup``) collapses near-duplicate claim atoms BEFORE
  29: #1/#2/#5 so a verbose report cannot inflate by restating one easy fact. Applied
  30: identically to every system.
  31: """
  32: from __future__ import annotations
  33: 
  34: import json
  35: from dataclasses import dataclass
  36: from pathlib import Path
  37: 
  38: from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import ClaimRow, RubricElement
  39: from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_dedup import dedup_claims
  40: 
  41: _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2")
  42: 
  43: # Default location of the pre-registered safety-floor element registry. Lives under
  44: # tracked config/ (NOT runtime outputs/, which is gitignored) — it is hand-authored
  45: # pre-registration, committed, not a regenerated artifact.
  46: _SAFETY_FLOOR_REGISTRY = (
  47:     Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
  48:     / "config" / "dr_benchmark" / "safety_floor_elements_v3.json"
  49: )
  50: 
  51: _DIVERSITY_NOTE = (
  52:     "diversity_score is a support-concentration DIAGNOSTIC (distinct sources among "
  53:     "VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED count), NOT a superiority signal; a higher value is "
  54:     "NOT a §-1.1 'win' (unique-source counts are banned as quality signals)."
  55: )
  56: 
  57: 
  58: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  59: class ScoredClaim:
  60:     """One audited claim paired with its prose text (text is used ONLY for dedup —
  61:     never for verdict assignment, which already happened upstream)."""
  62:     text: str
  63:     row: ClaimRow
  64: 
  65: 
  66: def _is_material(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
  67:     return row.severity in _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES
  68: 
  69: 
  70: def _has_resolved_citation(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
  71:     """A claim is citation-supported only if it carries a non-empty resolved
  72:     citation id (an unresolved / None citation does NOT count — traceability floor)."""
  73:     return bool(row.citation_id)
  74: 
  75: 
  76: def faithfulness_precision(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
  77:     material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
  78:     denom = len(material)
  79:     verified = sum(1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED")
  80:     return {
  81:         "material_atoms": denom,
  82:         "verified": verified,
  83:         "value": (verified / denom) if denom else None,
  84:     }
  85: 
  86: 
  87: def citation_support_rate(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
  88:     material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
  89:     denom = len(material)
  90:     supported = sum(
  91:         1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED" and _has_resolved_citation(r)
  92:     )
  93:     return {
  94:         "material_atoms": denom,
  95:         "verified_and_cited": supported,
  96:         "value": (supported / denom) if denom else None,
  97:     }
  98: 
  99: 
 100: def diversity_score(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
 101:     verified = [r for r in rows if r.verdict == "VERIFIED"]
 102:     denom = len(verified)
 103:     distinct_sources = {r.citation_id for r in verified if r.citation_id}
 104:     return {
 105:         "verified_claims": denom,
 106:         "distinct_sources": len(distinct_sources),
 107:         "value": (len(distinct_sources) / denom) if denom else None,
 108:         "note": _DIVERSITY_NOTE,
 109:     }
 110: 
 111: 
 112: def required_entity_recall(rubric: list[RubricElement] | None) -> dict:
 113:     if not rubric:
 114:         return {"total_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
 115:                 "pending": True}
 116:     total = len(rubric)
 117:     covered_supported = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered and e.citation_supported)
 118:     return {
 119:         "total_required": total,
 120:         "covered_supported": covered_supported,
 121:         "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
 122:         "missing": [e.element_id for e in rubric
 123:                     if not (e.covered and e.citation_supported)],
 124:         "pending": False,
 125:     }
 126: 
 127: 
 128: def safety_floor_recall(
 129:     rubric: list[RubricElement] | None, safety_element_ids: set[str] | None,
 130: ) -> dict:
 131:     if not rubric or not safety_element_ids:
 132:         return {"total_safety_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
 133:                 "pending": True}
 134:     # Denominator = the PRE-REGISTERED tagged count, NOT the count present in the
 135:     # supplied rubric (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2): a tagged id missing from the rubric
 136:     # must SURFACE + count AGAINST recall (fail-safe), never silently shrink the
 137:     # denominator into a falsely-high recall.
 138:     rubric_by_id = {e.element_id: e for e in rubric}
 139:     total = len(safety_element_ids)
 140:     covered_supported = 0
 141:     missing: list[str] = []
 142:     missing_from_rubric: list[str] = []
 143:     for eid in sorted(safety_element_ids):
 144:         e = rubric_by_id.get(eid)
 145:         if e is None:
 146:             missing_from_rubric.append(eid)
 147:             missing.append(eid)
 148:             continue
 149:         if e.covered and e.citation_supported:
 150:             covered_supported += 1
 151:         else:
 152:             missing.append(eid)
 153:     return {
 154:         "total_safety_required": total,
 155:         "covered_supported": covered_supported,
 156:         "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
 157:         "missing": missing,
 158:         "missing_from_rubric": missing_from_rubric,
 159:         "pending": False,
 160:     }
 161: 
 162: 
 163: def load_safety_floor_element_ids(
 164:     question_id: str, *, registry_path: Path | None = None,
 165: ) -> set[str]:
 166:     """Read the pre-registered safety-floor element ids for one question from
 167:     ``safety_floor_elements_v3.json`` (transparent tagging of the frozen rubric;
 168:     NOT invented claims). Missing file / question → empty set (safety_floor_recall
 169:     then reports pending, never a false 0)."""
 170:     path = registry_path or _SAFETY_FLOOR_REGISTRY
 171:     if not path.exists():
 172:         return set()
 173:     try:
 174:         data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 175:     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
 176:         return set()
 177:     qmap = data.get("questions", {})
 178:     entry = qmap.get(str(question_id)) or qmap.get(f"Q{question_id}")
 179:     if not entry:
 180:         return set()
 181:     return {str(eid) for eid in entry.get("safety_floor_element_ids", [])}
 182: 
 183: 
 184: def _verdict_aware_keep(
 185:     scored_claims: list[ScoredClaim], groups: list[list[int]],
 186: ) -> tuple[list[ClaimRow], int]:
 187:     """Collapse ONLY repeated VERIFIED claims; keep EVERY non-VERIFIED claim
 188:     (Codex brief-gate iter-1 P2: a dedup must never hide a bad verdict — if a
 189:     cluster mixes a VERIFIED restatement with an UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/PARTIAL/
 190:     UNREACHABLE near-duplicate, the bad verdict survives uncollapsed).
 191: 
 192:     Within each text cluster: keep the FIRST VERIFIED row (collapsing the rest of
 193:     the VERIFIED restatements to one verified unit) PLUS every non-VERIFIED row
 194:     untouched. Returns (kept_rows, n_verified_collapsed)."""
 195:     kept: list[ClaimRow] = []
 196:     n_verified_collapsed = 0
 197:     for cluster in groups:
 198:         rows_in = [scored_claims[i].row for i in cluster]
 199:         verified = [r for r in rows_in if r.verdict == "VERIFIED"]
 200:         non_verified = [r for r in rows_in if r.verdict != "VERIFIED"]
 201:         if verified:
 202:             # Deterministic, disclosed representative choice (Codex brief-gate iter-2
 203:             # P2): when VERIFIED duplicates have mixed citation resolution, keep the
 204:             # FIRST one carrying a RESOLVED citation so citation_support_rate reflects
 205:             # the best-supported instance; else keep the first VERIFIED. Stable by
 206:             # original cluster order.
 207:             rep = next((r for r in verified if r.citation_id), verified[0])
 208:             kept.append(rep)
 209:             n_verified_collapsed += len(verified) - 1
 210:         kept.extend(non_verified)
 211:     return kept, n_verified_collapsed
 212: 
 213: 
 214: def compute_extended_metrics(
 215:     scored_claims: list[ScoredClaim],
 216:     *,
 217:     rubric: list[RubricElement] | None = None,
 218:     safety_element_ids: set[str] | None = None,
 219:     min_jaccard: float = 0.80,
 220: ) -> dict:
 221:     """The 5 metrics for ONE (system × question). Dedups REPEATED VERIFIED claim
 222:     atoms first (anti-inflation, never hiding a bad verdict), then rolls up over the
 223:     kept ClaimRows + the audited frozen rubric. Pure aggregation — assigns no
 224:     verdicts, touches no gate.
 225: 
 226:     ``scored_claims`` is a list of ``ScoredClaim(text, row)`` — a typed AUDITED
 227:     carrier (the claim's prose text paired with its already-reconciled ``ClaimRow``).
 228:     The text is used ONLY for the Claimify dedup; every metric value is derived from
 229:     the ``ClaimRow`` verdicts + the rubric, never from the text (the §-1.1 guard)."""
 230:     texts = [sc.text for sc in scored_claims]
 231:     dedup = dedup_claims(texts, min_jaccard=min_jaccard)
 232:     kept_rows, n_verified_collapsed = _verdict_aware_keep(scored_claims, dedup.groups)
 233: 
 234:     return {
 235:         "n_raw_claims": len(scored_claims),
 236:         "n_kept_claims": len(kept_rows),
 237:         "dedup": {
 238:             "n_text_clusters_collapsed": dedup.n_collapsed,
 239:             "n_verified_collapsed": n_verified_collapsed,
 240:             "collapsed_groups": dedup.collapsed_groups(),
 241:             "min_jaccard": min_jaccard,
 242:             "policy": "collapse repeated VERIFIED only; every non-VERIFIED claim kept "
 243:                       "(a bad verdict is never hidden by a VERIFIED near-duplicate).",
 244:         },
 245:         "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(kept_rows),
 246:         "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(kept_rows),
 247:         "diversity_score": diversity_score(kept_rows),
 248:         "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric),
 249:         "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric, safety_element_ids),
 250:         "methodology_note": (
 251:             "all metrics derived from the per-claim audit ledger (VERIFIED verdicts "
 252:             "vs FETCHED cited spans) + the frozen gold rubric; NEVER from raw report "
 253:             "text; dedup applied identically to every system; NOT a superiority claim."
 254:         ),
 255:     }

 100: def score_one(
 101:     *, system: str, question_id: str, rubric_path: Path, ledger_path: Path,
 102:     run_dir: Path | None = None,
 103: ) -> dict:
 104:     """Score one (system, question). Returns the scored dict; pure (no I/O beyond reads)."""
 105:     polaris_gate_identity: dict | None = None
 106:     if system == "polaris":
 107:         if run_dir is None:
 108:             raise InvalidRunError("polaris scoring requires --run-dir")
 109:         _check_polaris_gate(run_dir)
 110:         # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2: surface the gate's served-identity + reachability for the
 111:         # aggregator's REQUIRED identity-pins block (design answer F).
 112:         polaris_gate_identity = _read_polaris_gate_identity(run_dir)
 113: 
 114:     ledger = load_ledger(ledger_path)
 115:     # Codex PR-3 diff P1 #1: require a RECONCILED ledger; single-auditor ledgers must not
 116:     # be scored directly (that would bypass the dual-§-1.1 conservative-MAX discipline).
 117:     if ledger.auditor != "reconciled":
 118:         raise ValueError(
 119:             f"ledger.auditor must be 'reconciled' (Claude+Codex dual audit), got "
 120:             f"{ledger.auditor!r}; pass the reconciled ledger from "
 121:             f"scripts.dr_benchmark.reconcile, not a single-auditor ledger"
 122:         )
 123:     if ledger.system != system:
 124:         raise ValueError(
 125:             f"ledger.system {ledger.system!r} != requested system {system!r}"
 126:         )
 127:     if ledger.question_id != question_id:
 128:         raise ValueError(
 129:             f"ledger.question_id {ledger.question_id!r} != requested {question_id!r}"
 130:         )
 131: 
 132:     rubric_doc = json.loads(rubric_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 133:     question_block = next(
 134:         (q for q in rubric_doc["questions"] if q["question_id"] == question_id), None,
 135:     )
 136:     if question_block is None:
 137:         raise ValueError(f"rubric does not contain question_id={question_id!r}")
 138:     if rubric_doc.get("rubric_sha256") != ledger.rubric_sha256:
 139:         raise ValueError(
 140:             "rubric_sha256 mismatch — ledger was audited against a DIFFERENT pinned rubric: "
 141:             f"ledger={ledger.rubric_sha256}, rubric_doc={rubric_doc.get('rubric_sha256')}"
 142:         )
 143: 
 144:     # Build ClaimRow[] from ledger.claims (mirrors validation; ledger already validated).
 145:     rows = [
 146:         ClaimRow(
 147:             claim_id=c.claim_id,
 148:             severity=c.severity,
 149:             verdict=c.verdict,
 150:             citation_id=c.citation_id,
 151:             span_quote=c.span_quote,
 152:             unreachable_subtype=c.unreachable_subtype,
 153:             audit_note=c.audit_note,
 154:         )
 155:         for c in ledger.claims
 156:     ]
 157:     # Map coverage rows to RubricElement[] (the scorer expects covered+citation_supported).
 158:     coverage_by_eid = {cv.element_id: cv for cv in ledger.coverage}
 159:     rubric_elements = []
 160:     for el in question_block["elements"]:
 161:         cv = coverage_by_eid.get(el["element_id"])
 162:         if cv is None:
 163:             # No coverage row for a required element = NOT covered (conservative).
 164:             rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
 165:                 element_id=el["element_id"], covered=False, citation_supported=False,
 166:             ))
 167:         else:
 168:             rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
 169:                 element_id=el["element_id"],
 170:                 covered=cv.covered,
 171:                 citation_supported=cv.citation_supported,
 172:             ))
 173: 
 174:     result = system_passes_question(rows, rubric_elements)
 175:     out = {
 176:         "system": system,
 177:         "question_id": question_id,
 178:         "rubric_sha256": rubric_doc["rubric_sha256"],
 179:         "ledger_audit_method": ledger.audit_method,
 180:         "n_claims_material": result["lane1"]["material_atoms"],
 181:         "n_rubric_elements": len(rubric_elements),
 182:         "passed": result["passed"],
 183:         "reasons": result["reasons"],
 184:         "lane1": result["lane1"],
 185:         "lane2": result["lane2"],
 186:     }
 187:     # I-perm-024 (#1216): wire the extended claim-by-claim metrics into the LIVE scorer
 188:     # (the wiring audit found run_scorecard.py is never invoked in the live path — this is).
 189:     # Default-OFF PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS -> byte-identical when off. Every metric is derived
 190:     # ONLY from the audited ledger rows + the frozen rubric, NEVER raw report text (§-1.1). The
 191:     # Claimify dedup is intentionally SKIPPED: the reconciled ledger's claims are already atomic
 192:     # and unique by claim_id (the dual-§-1.1 audit deduplicates), so there is no inflation vector
 193:     # to collapse and the ledger carries no per-claim text to dedup on.
 194:     if os.environ.get("PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS", "").strip().lower() in (
 195:         "1", "true", "yes", "on",
 196:     ):
 197:         from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.extended_metrics import (
 198:             citation_support_rate,
 199:             diversity_score,
 200:             faithfulness_precision,
 201:             load_safety_floor_element_ids,
 202:             required_entity_recall,
 203:             safety_floor_recall,
 204:         )
 205:         _safety_ids = load_safety_floor_element_ids(question_id)
 206:         out["extended"] = {
 207:             "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(rows),
 208:             "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(rows),
 209:             "diversity_score": diversity_score(rows),
 210:             "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric_elements),
 211:             "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric_elements, _safety_ids),
 212:             "dedup_applied": False,
 213:             "methodology_note": (
 214:                 "derived from the reconciled dual-§-1.1 audit ledger rows (already atomic/"
 215:                 "unique by claim_id) + the frozen rubric; NEVER from raw report text; "
 216:                 "diversity_score is a diagnostic, NOT a superiority signal."
 217:             ),
 218:         }
 219:     if polaris_gate_identity is not None:
 220:         out["pathB_gate_identity"] = polaris_gate_identity
 221:     return out
 222:

 224: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
 225:     p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Path-B (system, question) scorer.")
 226:     p.add_argument("--system", required=True, choices=("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"))
 227:     p.add_argument("--question", required=True, dest="question_id",
 228:                    choices=("Q72", "Q75", "Q76", "Q78", "Q90"))
 229:     p.add_argument("--rubric", required=True, type=Path,
 230:                    help="frozen-rubric JSON snapshot (rubric_v3_frozen.json)")
 231:     p.add_argument("--ledger", required=True, type=Path,
 232:                    help="reconciled dual-audit ledger JSON (claude+codex)")
 233:     p.add_argument("--run-dir", type=Path, default=None,
 234:                    help="polaris run dir (required for system=polaris; gate-check)")
 235:     p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path,
 236:                    help="path to write scored JSON")
 237:     args = p.parse_args(argv)
 238: 
 239:     try:
 240:         scored = score_one(
 241:             system=args.system, question_id=args.question_id,
 242:             rubric_path=args.rubric, ledger_path=args.ledger,
 243:             run_dir=args.run_dir,
 244:         )
 245:     except InvalidRunError as exc:
 246:         print(f"[score_run] INVALID: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
 247:         # Write a stub INVALID record so the aggregator can still account for it.
 248:         args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 249:         args.out.write_text(json.dumps({
 250:             "system": args.system, "question_id": args.question_id,
 251:             "passed": False, "invalid": True, "reason": str(exc),
 252:         }, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
 253:         return 2  # distinct from generic error
 254:     except Exception as exc:
 255:         print(f"[score_run] ERROR: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
 256:         return 1
 257: 
 258:     args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 259:     args.out.write_text(
 260:         json.dumps(scored, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
 261:         encoding="utf-8",
 262:     )
 263:     return 0

 267:     sys.exit(main())

 succeeded in 345067ms:
   1: """Two-lane claim-audit scorer for the DR head-to-head (I-safety-002b / #925).
   2: 
   3: REPLACES the §-1.1-banned/rigged `beat_both_scorer.py` + `dimension_scorers.py` (count /
   4: pattern / string-match scoring + POLARIS auto-win dimensions). This scorer is a PURE
   5: AGGREGATOR over the reconciled per-claim audit LEDGER produced by the Claude+Codex dual
   6: §-1.1 line-by-line audit (claim → fetched cited span → verdict). It assigns NO verdicts
   7: itself and gives POLARIS no free pass — every system's ledger is scored identically.
   8: 
   9: Lane 1 = claim faithfulness (against the FETCHED cited span).
  10: Lane 2 = pre-registered gold-rubric coverage (independent sources), each covered point
  11:          itself citation-supported — so a terse report cannot win on faithfulness alone.
  12: 
  13: Pure logic; fixture-tested with no model. Per plan `.codex/I-safety-002b/execution_plan_pathB.md`
  14: §5/§6 (Codex APPROVE iter 5).
  15: """
  16: 
  17: from __future__ import annotations
  18: 
  19: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  20: from typing import Literal
  21: 
  22: Verdict = Literal["VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE"]
  23: Severity = Literal["S0", "S1", "S2", "S3"]
  24: UnreachableSubtype = Literal[
  25:     "paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing", "metadata_only",
  26: ]
  27: 
  28: # Material = decision-relevant (S0-S2). S3 is observe-only, NOT scored (plan §5).
  29: _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2")
  30: # "unsupported-or-worse" verdicts that count against faithfulness.
  31: _BAD_VERDICTS = ("PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE")
  32: _PARTIAL_WEIGHT = 0.5  # pre-registered (plan §5)
  33: _COVERAGE_THRESHOLD = 0.70  # literal, frozen (plan §5)
  34: 
  35: 
  36: @dataclass
  37: class ClaimRow:
  38:     """One reconciled audit verdict for one atomic claim (from the dual line-by-line)."""
  39: 
  40:     claim_id: str
  41:     severity: Severity
  42:     verdict: Verdict
  43:     citation_id: str | None        # the citation the claim attaches to (None = uncited)
  44:     span_quote: str | None         # exact supporting/refuting span from the FETCHED source
  45:     unreachable_subtype: UnreachableSubtype | None = None
  46:     audit_note: str | None = None  # e.g. "no supporting span found in cited source" for UNSUPPORTED
  47: 
  48:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
  49:         if self.verdict == "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is None:
  50:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNREACHABLE requires an unreachable_subtype")
  51:         if self.verdict != "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is not None:
  52:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype only valid for UNREACHABLE")
  53:         # FABRICATED/PARTIAL must quote the refuting/partial span.
  54:         if self.verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not self.span_quote:
  55:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: {self.verdict} requires a span_quote (evidence)")
  56:         # UNSUPPORTED on a CITED claim must record WHY (span checked or explicit no-support note)
  57:         # so the verdict is traceable, not asserted (Codex P2).
  58:         if self.verdict == "UNSUPPORTED" and self.citation_id and not (self.span_quote or self.audit_note):
  59:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNSUPPORTED+cited requires a span_quote or audit_note (traceability)")
  60: 
  61:     @property
  62:     def is_material(self) -> bool:
  63:         return self.severity in _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES
  64: 
  65: 
  66: @dataclass
  67: class RubricElement:
  68:     """One pre-registered gold-rubric required element (from independent sources)."""
  69: 
  70:     element_id: str
  71:     covered: bool             # did the report cover this required element?
  72:     citation_supported: bool  # AND is the covered point itself citation-supported?
  73: 
  74: 
  75: def lane1_faithfulness(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
  76:     """S0-S2 material unsupported-or-worse rate + per-verdict breakdown. Positive = bad."""
  77:     material = [r for r in rows if r.is_material]
  78:     denom = len(material)
  79:     counts: dict[str, int] = {v: 0 for v in ("VERIFIED", *_BAD_VERDICTS)}
  80:     for r in material:
  81:         counts[r.verdict] += 1
  82:     # PARTIAL weighted 0.5; UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE full weight.
  83:     weighted_bad = (
  84:         counts["UNSUPPORTED"] + counts["FABRICATED"] + counts["UNREACHABLE"]
  85:         + _PARTIAL_WEIGHT * counts["PARTIAL"]
  86:     )
  87:     rate = (weighted_bad / denom) if denom else 0.0
  88:     return {
  89:         "material_atoms": denom,
  90:         "verdict_counts": counts,
  91:         "weighted_unsupported_or_worse": weighted_bad,
  92:         "unsupported_or_worse_rate": rate,
  93:         "hard_fail_count": counts["UNSUPPORTED"] + counts["FABRICATED"],  # the gating verdicts
  94:         "observed_S3_excluded": sum(1 for r in rows if r.severity == "S3"),
  95:     }
  96: 
  97: 
  98: def lane2_coverage(rubric: list[RubricElement]) -> dict:
  99:     """Covered-AND-citation-supported / total required elements (plan §5/§6)."""
 100:     total = len(rubric)
 101:     covered_supported = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered and e.citation_supported)
 102:     covered_only = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered)
 103:     frac = (covered_supported / total) if total else 0.0
 104:     return {
 105:         "total_required": total,
 106:         "covered": covered_only,
 107:         "covered_and_citation_supported": covered_supported,
 108:         "coverage_fraction": frac,
 109:         "missing": [e.element_id for e in rubric if not (e.covered and e.citation_supported)],
 110:     }
 111: 
 112: 
 113: def system_passes_question(rows: list[ClaimRow], rubric: list[RubricElement]) -> dict:
 114:     """A system PASSES a question iff zero S0-S2 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED material claims AND
 115:     coverage >= 0.70 (plan §5). Returns the verdict + the reasons (every gate explicit)."""
 116:     l1 = lane1_faithfulness(rows)
 117:     l2 = lane2_coverage(rubric)
 118:     zero_hard_fail = l1["hard_fail_count"] == 0
 119:     meets_coverage = l2["coverage_fraction"] >= _COVERAGE_THRESHOLD
 120:     passed = zero_hard_fail and meets_coverage
 121:     reasons = []
 122:     if not zero_hard_fail:
 123:         reasons.append(f"{l1['hard_fail_count']} S0-S2 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED material claim(s)")
 124:     if not meets_coverage:
 125:         reasons.append(f"coverage {l2['coverage_fraction']:.2f} < {_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}")
 126:     return {"passed": passed, "reasons": reasons, "lane1": l1, "lane2": l2}
 127: 
 128: 
 129: @dataclass
 130: class SystemQuestionLedger:
 131:     """The scored audit ledger for one (system × question). NOT a 'BEAT-BOTH' scoreboard."""
 132: 
 133:     system: str         # "polaris" | "chatgpt" | "gemini" | "perplexity"
 134:     question_id: str
 135:     rows: list[ClaimRow]
 136:     rubric: list[RubricElement]
 137:     result: dict = field(init=False)
 138: 
 139:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
 140:         self.result = system_passes_question(self.rows, self.rubric)
 141: 
 142: 
 143: def aggregate(ledgers: list[SystemQuestionLedger]) -> dict:
 144:     """Per-system aggregate across questions. Every number traces to claim/span evidence in
 145:     the per-(system×question) ledgers; NO cross-system 'wins' headline (plan §8)."""
 146:     by_system: dict[str, dict] = {}
 147:     for lg in ledgers:
 148:         s = by_system.setdefault(lg.system, {"questions": 0, "passed": 0, "rates": [], "hard_fails": 0})
 149:         s["questions"] += 1
 150:         s["passed"] += int(lg.result["passed"])
 151:         s["rates"].append(lg.result["lane1"]["unsupported_or_worse_rate"])
 152:         s["hard_fails"] += lg.result["lane1"]["hard_fail_count"]
 153:     for s in by_system.values():
 154:         s["mean_unsupported_or_worse_rate"] = (sum(s["rates"]) / len(s["rates"])) if s["rates"] else 0.0
 155:     return {"by_system": by_system, "n_ledgers": len(ledgers), "note": "pilot; per-claim traceable; not a superiority claim"}

codex
The scorer builds `rows` and `rubric_elements` before calling `system_passes_question`, and the new block only adds `out["extended"]` after that result is already copied. I’m checking how the scored JSON is consumed so a new key cannot become a hidden gate through aggregation.

   1: """Path-B dual-§-1.1-audit ledger schema (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
   2: 
   3: The per-(system × question) JSON each auditor (Claude / Codex) writes for the dual line-by-line
   4: audit. Mirrors `src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer.ClaimRow` validation so a ledger
   5: that fails-closed here cannot smuggle a malformed row into the scorer. Pure stdlib + dataclasses
   6: (no pydantic dep added).
   7: 
   8: Public surface:
   9: - ``Claim``       — one atomic claim audited against the FETCHED cited span.
  10: - ``Coverage``    — one pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage by THIS system's report.
  11: - ``Ledger``      — the full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor.
  12: - ``load_ledger`` — read+validate a ledger JSON from disk.
  13: - ``dump_ledger`` — write a validated ledger JSON to disk.
  14: """

  20: from pathlib import Path
  21: from typing import Literal
  22: 
  23: # Mirrored from claim_audit_scorer.py (PR-1) — do NOT diverge.
  24: Verdict = Literal["VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE"]
  25: Severity = Literal["S0", "S1", "S2", "S3"]
  26: UnreachableSubtype = Literal["paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing"]
  27: Auditor = Literal["claude", "codex", "reconciled"]
  28: SystemId = Literal["polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"]
  29: 
  30: _VERDICTS = ("VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE")
  31: _SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2", "S3")
  32: _UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES = ("paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing")
  33: # "reconciled" is the output of dual-auditor reconciliation (PR-3 reconcile.py), NOT a
  34: # real auditor — the upstream auditors are recorded in audit_method.
  35: _AUDITORS = ("claude", "codex", "reconciled")
  36: _SYSTEMS = ("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini")
  37: 
  38: 
  39: @dataclass
  40: class Claim:
  41:     """One atomic claim's audit row. Validation MIRRORS ClaimRow.__post_init__:
  42:     UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype; FABRICATED/PARTIAL require span_quote;
  43:     UNSUPPORTED+cited requires span_quote OR audit_note (traceability)."""
  44: 
  45:     claim_id: str
  46:     severity: str            # "S0"|"S1"|"S2"|"S3"
  47:     verdict: str             # one of _VERDICTS
  48:     citation_id: str | None = None
  49:     span_quote: str | None = None
  50:     unreachable_subtype: str | None = None
  51:     audit_note: str | None = None
  52: 
  53:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
  54:         if not self.claim_id:
  55:             raise ValueError("Claim.claim_id is required")
  56:         if self.severity not in _SEVERITIES:
  57:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: severity {self.severity!r} not in {_SEVERITIES}")
  58:         if self.verdict not in _VERDICTS:
  59:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: verdict {self.verdict!r} not in {_VERDICTS}")
  60:         if self.verdict == "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is None:
  61:             raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNREACHABLE requires unreachable_subtype")
  62:         if self.verdict != "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is not None:
  63:             raise ValueError(
  64:                 f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype only valid for UNREACHABLE"
  65:             )
  66:         if (
  67:             self.unreachable_subtype is not None
  68:             and self.unreachable_subtype not in _UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES
  69:         ):
  70:             raise ValueError(
  71:                 f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype {self.unreachable_subtype!r} "
  72:                 f"not in {_UNREACHABLE_SUBTYPES}"
  73:             )
  74:         if self.verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not self.span_quote:
  75:             raise ValueError(
  76:                 f"{self.claim_id}: {self.verdict} requires a span_quote (the refuting/partial span)"
  77:             )
  78:         if (
  79:             self.verdict == "UNSUPPORTED"
  80:             and self.citation_id
  81:             and not (self.span_quote or self.audit_note)
  82:         ):
  83:             raise ValueError(
  84:                 f"{self.claim_id}: UNSUPPORTED+cited requires a span_quote or audit_note (traceability)"
  85:             )
  86: 
  87: 
  88: @dataclass
  89: class Coverage:
  90:     """One pre-registered gold-rubric element's coverage row, produced in the SAME
  91:     pass as the per-claim audit (Codex PR-3 design answer B)."""
  92: 
  93:     element_id: str
  94:     covered: bool
  95:     citation_supported: bool
  96:     auditor_note: str | None = None
  97: 
  98:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
  99:         if not self.element_id:
 100:             raise ValueError("Coverage.element_id is required")
 101:         # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #1: enforce real bools. JSON deserialization can yield strings
 102:         # ("false", "0", "") that are truthy in Python and would silently pass scoring.
 103:         for fname in ("covered", "citation_supported"):
 104:             v = getattr(self, fname)
 105:             if not isinstance(v, bool):
 106:                 raise ValueError(
 107:                     f"Coverage.{fname} must be a bool, got {type(v).__name__}: {v!r}"
 108:                 )
 109: 
 110: 
 111: @dataclass
 112: class Ledger:
 113:     """The full (system, question) audit ledger written by ONE auditor (claude OR codex)."""
 114: 
 115:     system: str           # one of _SYSTEMS
 116:     question_id: str      # "Q72"|"Q75"|"Q76"|"Q78"|"Q90"
 117:     auditor: str          # one of _AUDITORS
 118:     audit_method: str     # short description, e.g. "dual-§-1.1-line-by-line-2026-05-28"
 119:     audit_timestamp_utc: str
 120:     rubric_sha256: str    # the freeze_pin.txt SHA of the rubric being audited against
 121:     claims: list[Claim] = field(default_factory=list)
 122:     coverage: list[Coverage] = field(default_factory=list)
 123: 
 124:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
 125:         if self.system not in _SYSTEMS:
 126:             raise ValueError(f"system {self.system!r} not in {_SYSTEMS}")
 127:         if self.auditor not in _AUDITORS:
 128:             raise ValueError(f"auditor {self.auditor!r} not in {_AUDITORS}")
 129:         if not self.question_id:
 130:             raise ValueError("Ledger.question_id is required")
 131:         if not self.audit_method:
 132:             raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_method is required (audit-protocol identity)")
 133:         if not self.audit_timestamp_utc:
 134:             raise ValueError("Ledger.audit_timestamp_utc is required (audit-time identity)")
 135:         if not self.rubric_sha256:
 136:             raise ValueError("Ledger.rubric_sha256 is required (pre-registration anchor)")
 137:         # Duplicate detection: claim_id and element_id are unique within a ledger.
 138:         seen_claims = set()
 139:         for c in self.claims:
 140:             if c.claim_id in seen_claims:
 141:                 raise ValueError(f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r}")
 142:             seen_claims.add(c.claim_id)
 143:         seen_elements = set()
 144:         for cv in self.coverage:
 145:             if cv.element_id in seen_elements:
 146:                 raise ValueError(f"duplicate element_id {cv.element_id!r}")
 147:             seen_elements.add(cv.element_id)
 148: 
 149: 
 150: def load_ledger(path: Path) -> Ledger:
 151:     """Read a ledger JSON; validate per dataclass __post_init__; raise on any malformed row."""
 152:     raw = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 153:     claims = [Claim(**c) for c in raw.get("claims", [])]
 154:     coverage = [Coverage(**c) for c in raw.get("coverage", [])]
 155:     head = {k: v for k, v in raw.items() if k not in ("claims", "coverage")}
 156:     return Ledger(claims=claims, coverage=coverage, **head)
 157: 
 158: 
 159: def dump_ledger(ledger: Ledger, path: Path) -> None:
 160:     """Write a ledger JSON (validated by construction; no extra validation needed)."""
 161:     out = asdict(ledger)
 162:     Path(path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 163:     Path(path).write_text(
 164:         json.dumps(out, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
 165:         encoding="utf-8",
 166:     )

  17: 
  18: import argparse
  19: import json
  20: import sys
  21: from pathlib import Path
  22: 
  23: _CLINICAL_QUESTIONS = ("Q75", "Q76", "Q78")
  24: _ALL_QUESTIONS = ("Q72", "Q75", "Q76", "Q78", "Q90")
  25: _SYSTEMS = ("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini")
  26: 
  27: 
  28: def _read_scored(path: Path) -> dict:
  29:     return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
  30: 
  31: 
  32: def _collect(scored_dir: Path) -> dict[str, dict[str, dict]]:
  33:     """Return {system: {question_id: scored_dict}} from scored_dir."""
  34:     out: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = {s: {} for s in _SYSTEMS}
  35:     for f in sorted(scored_dir.glob("*.json")):
  36:         d = _read_scored(f)
  37:         sys_id = d.get("system")
  38:         qid = d.get("question_id")
  39:         if sys_id in out and qid:
  40:             out[sys_id][qid] = d
  41:     return out
  42: 
  43: 
  44: def _section(scored: dict[str, dict[str, dict]], question_ids: tuple[str, ...], label: str) -> list[str]:
  45:     """Render a section table for the given question subset."""
  46:     lines = [f"### {label}\n"]
  47:     lines.append("| System | Valid runs | Passed | Pass rate | Invalid (reasons) |")
  48:     lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
  49:     for sys_id in _SYSTEMS:
  50:         valid: list[dict] = []
  51:         invalid: list[dict] = []
  52:         for qid in question_ids:
  53:             d = scored.get(sys_id, {}).get(qid)
  54:             if d is None:
  55:                 invalid.append({"question_id": qid, "reason": "no scored JSON"})
  56:                 continue
  57:             if d.get("invalid"):
  58:                 invalid.append({"question_id": qid, "reason": d.get("reason", "INVALID")})
  59:             else:
  60:                 valid.append(d)
  61:         passed = sum(1 for d in valid if d.get("passed"))
  62:         n_valid = len(valid)
  63:         rate = f"{passed}/{n_valid}" if n_valid else "0/0"
  64:         rate_pct = f"({100.0 * passed / n_valid:.0f}%)" if n_valid else "(n/a)"
  65:         invalid_str = "; ".join(
  66:             f"{x['question_id']}: {x['reason'][:80]}" for x in invalid
  67:         ) or "—"
  68:         lines.append(
  69:             f"| {_cell(sys_id)} | {n_valid} | {passed} | {_cell(rate)} {_cell(rate_pct)} | "
  70:             f"{_cell(invalid_str)} |"
  71:         )
  72:     lines.append("")
  73:     return lines
  74: 
  75: 
  76: def _cell(text: str) -> str:
  77:     """Escape characters that break markdown table cells (Codex PR-3 diff P3 #2)."""
  78:     return (
  79:         (text or "")
  80:         .replace("|", "\\|")
  81:         .replace("\r\n", " ")
  82:         .replace("\n", " ")
  83:         .replace("\r", " ")
  84:     )
  85: 
  86: 
  87: def _per_question_detail(scored: dict[str, dict[str, dict]]) -> list[str]:
  88:     lines = ["### Per-question detail (clinical-3 + overall-5)\n"]
  89:     for qid in _ALL_QUESTIONS:
  90:         lines.append(f"#### {qid}\n")
  91:         lines.append("| System | Passed | Lane-1 hard_fail | Lane-2 coverage | Reasons |")
  92:         lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
  93:         for sys_id in _SYSTEMS:
  94:             d = scored.get(sys_id, {}).get(qid)
  95:             if d is None:
  96:                 lines.append(f"| {sys_id} | — | — | — | no scored JSON |")
  97:                 continue
  98:             if d.get("invalid"):
  99:                 lines.append(
 100:                     f"| {sys_id} | INVALID | — | — | {_cell(str(d.get('reason', ''))[:120])} |"
 101:                 )
 102:                 continue
 103:             lane1 = d.get("lane1", {})
 104:             lane2 = d.get("lane2", {})
 105:             reasons = "; ".join(d.get("reasons", [])) or "passed"
 106:             lines.append(
 107:                 f"| {sys_id} | {d.get('passed')} | "
 108:                 f"{lane1.get('hard_fail_count', '?')} | "
 109:                 f"{lane2.get('coverage_fraction', 0):.2f} | "
 110:                 f"{_cell(reasons[:120])} |"
 111:             )
 112:         lines.append("")
 113:     return lines

 116: def _identity_pins_block(freeze_pin: Path, scored: dict[str, dict[str, dict]]) -> list[str]:
 117:     """Identity pins block (Codex answer F yes-required) — cite freeze_pin SHAs AND, per
 118:     Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2, the pathB_gate served-identity + reachability proofs from each

 128:

 130:     polaris = scored.get("polaris", {})

 147:             ev = pins_by_role.get("evaluator", {}) or {}

 152:             ev_str = (
 153:                 f"{ev.get('provider_name', '?')}/{ev.get('model_slug', '?')} "
 154:                 f"surrogate={(served.get('evaluator') or '?')[:12]}…"
 155:             )
 156:             lines.append(

 160:                 f"{_cell(str(ident.get('openrouter_provider_order') or '—'))} |"
 161:             )
 162:         lines.append("")
 163:     return lines

 166: def render_final_report(
 167:     *, scored_dir: Path, freeze_pin: Path, out_path: Path,
 168: ) -> int:
 169:     scored = _collect(scored_dir)
 170:     lines: list[str] = []
 171:     lines.append("# Path-B DR head-to-head — final report (I-safety-002b / #925)\n")
 172:     lines.append(

 176:         "label discipline. No 'wins' headline; every cell traces to a scored JSON.\n",
 177:     )

 181:     lines.extend(_section(scored, _ALL_QUESTIONS, "Overall-5 (#72/#75/#76/#78/#90)"))
 182:     lines.extend(_per_question_detail(scored))
 183:     lines.append(
 184:         "## Notes\n\n"
 185:         "- INVALID runs are reported and OMITTED from the numerator/denominator of the "
 186:         "valid subset (Codex PR-3 design answer D `report-omit`).\n"
 187:         "- Pass = zero S0–S2 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED material claims AND coverage ≥0.70 "

 190:         "`pathB_gate_INVALID` sentinel. Gate FAILURES are reported as INVALID with the "
 191:         "gate's reason.\n"
 192:     )
 193:     out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 194:     out_path.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
 195:     print(f"[aggregate_systems] wrote {out_path}")
 196:     return 0

1678:     # ORDER above is the fix; preflight_import_time_constants() is the assertion the subprocess test runs.

1688:                 args.upload_file, args.upload_classification

+    # I-perm-024 (#1216): wire the extended claim-by-claim metrics into the LIVE scorer
+    # (the wiring audit found run_scorecard.py is never invoked in the live path — this is).
+    # Default-OFF PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS -> byte-identical when off. Every metric is derived
+    # ONLY from the audited ledger rows + the frozen rubric, NEVER raw report text (§-1.1). The
+    # Claimify dedup is intentionally SKIPPED: the reconciled ledger's claims are already atomic
+    # and unique by claim_id (the dual-§-1.1 audit deduplicates), so there is no inflation vector
+    # to collapse and the ledger carries no per-claim text to dedup on.

+        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
+    ):

+            citation_support_rate,
+            diversity_score,
+            faithfulness_precision,
+            load_safety_floor_element_ids,
+            required_entity_recall,
+            safety_floor_recall,
+        )
+        _safety_ids = load_safety_floor_element_ids(question_id)
+        out["extended"] = {
+            "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(rows),
+            "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(rows),
+            "diversity_score": diversity_score(rows),
+            "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric_elements),
+            "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric_elements, _safety_ids),
+            "dedup_applied": False,
+            "methodology_note": (
+                "derived from the reconciled dual-§-1.1 audit ledger rows (already atomic/"
+                "unique by claim_id) + the frozen rubric; NEVER from raw report text; "
+                "diversity_score is a diagnostic, NOT a superiority signal."
+            ),
+        }

     return out

I’ll use the isolated offline fixture through the same reconcile-to-`score_one` path. The check will only write temporary files under `C:\tmp` and report whether the `extended` key appears only when the flag is enabled.
exec
"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "@'
import json, os, tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import load_ledger, dump_ledger
from scripts.dr_benchmark.reconcile import reconcile
from scripts.dr_benchmark.score_run import score_one

fixture = Path('tests/fixtures/offline_e2e')
claude = load_ledger(fixture / 'synthetic_ledger_claude.json')
codex = load_ledger(fixture / 'synthetic_ledger_codex.json')
rec = reconcile(claude, codex)
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(dir=r'C:\\tmp') as td:
    rec_path = Path(td) / 'reconciled.json'
    dump_ledger(rec, rec_path)
    rubric = fixture / 'synthetic_rubric.json'
    os.environ.pop('PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS', None)
    off = score_one(system=rec.system, question_id=rec.question_id, rubric_path=rubric, ledger_path=rec_path)
    os.environ['PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS'] = '1'
    on = score_one(system=rec.system, question_id=rec.question_id, rubric_path=rubric, ledger_path=rec_path)
    on_wo_ext = dict(on)
    ext = on_wo_ext.pop('extended', None)
    print(json.dumps({
        'off_has_extended': 'extended' in off,
        'on_has_extended': ext is not None,
        'on_without_extended_equals_off': on_wo_ext == off,
        'extended_keys': sorted(ext.keys()) if ext else [],
        'passed_equal': on.get('passed') == off.get('passed'),
        'lane1_equal': on.get('lane1') == off.get('lane1'),
        'lane2_equal': on.get('lane2') == off.get('lane2'),
    }, indent=2, sort_keys=True))

from pathlib import Path

    dump_ledger(rec, rec_path)
    rubric = fixture / 'synthetic_rubric.json'
    os.environ.pop('PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS', None)
    off = score_one(system=rec.system, question_id=rec.question_id, rubric_path=rubric, ledger_path=rec_path)

codex
There is a real frozen rubric under `outputs/dr_benchmark`, but no quick read-only reconciled fixture surfaced. I’ll use a non-filesystem monkeypatch to feed `score_one` the in-memory reconciled ledger, which avoids the sandbox write issue and still exercises the new branch.
exec
"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "@'
import json, os
from pathlib import Path
from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import load_ledger
from scripts.dr_benchmark.reconcile import reconcile
import scripts.dr_benchmark.score_run as sr

fixture = Path('tests/fixtures/offline_e2e')
rec = reconcile(
    load_ledger(fixture / 'synthetic_ledger_claude.json'),
    load_ledger(fixture / 'synthetic_ledger_codex.json'),
)
orig = sr.load_ledger
sr.load_ledger = lambda _path: rec
try:
    rubric = fixture / 'synthetic_rubric.json'
    os.environ.pop('PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS', None)
    off = sr.score_one(system=rec.system, question_id=rec.question_id, rubric_path=rubric, ledger_path=Path('unused.json'))
    os.environ['PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS'] = '1'
    on = sr.score_one(system=rec.system, question_id=rec.question_id, rubric_path=rubric, ledger_path=Path('unused.json'))
finally:
    sr.load_ledger = orig
    os.environ.pop('PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS', None)
on_wo_ext = dict(on)
ext = on_wo_ext.pop('extended', None)
print(json.dumps({
    'off_has_extended': 'extended' in off,
    'on_has_extended': ext is not None,
    'on_without_extended_equals_off': on_wo_ext == off,
    'extended_keys': sorted(ext.keys()) if ext else [],
    'passed_equal': on.get('passed') == off.get('passed'),
    'lane1_equal': on.get('lane1') == off.get('lane1'),
    'lane2_equal': on.get('lane2') == off.get('lane2'),
}, indent=2, sort_keys=True))

"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "python -m pytest tests\\dr_benchmark\\test_extended_metrics_iperm024.py tests\\dr_benchmark\\test_pr3_pipeline.py -q" in C:\POLARIS
 succeeded in 16260ms:
   1: """I-perm-024 (#1216) — Claimify-style claim-atom dedup for the beat-both scorer.
   2: 
   3: Anti-inflation pass: BEFORE the extended metrics roll up over the per-claim audit
   4: ledger, collapse near-duplicate claim atoms so that N restatements of one obvious
   5: fact count ONCE. Without this, a verbose report could inflate
   6: ``faithfulness_precision`` / ``citation_support_rate`` / ``diversity_score`` simply
   7: by repeating the same easy claim — a §-1.1-banned "more text = better" loophole.
   8: 
   9: Pure text + deterministic. Operates on the claim TEXT only and returns index
  10: groups; the caller (``extended_metrics``) joins the kept indices back to its
  11: ``ClaimRow``s. Applied IDENTICALLY to every system (no POLARIS bias).
  12: 
  13: Merge rule (conservative — under-merge is safe, over-merge is the risk):
  14:   two claims merge iff
  15:     (a) their numeric signatures are COMPATIBLE (equal, or at least one empty, and
  16:         no populated-axis conflict — reuses the ``fact_dedup`` populated-axis lesson
  17:         so "3.7% in 2014" never merges with "3.7% in 2016"), AND
  18:     (b) their content-token Jaccard >= ``min_jaccard`` (default 0.80).
  19: The dedup is REPORTED (n_raw / n_kept / groups), never silent.
  20: 
  21: SOTA framing: Claimify (arXiv 2407.03572) decomposes + deduplicates claims before
  22: scoring; here we only need the dedup half over already-extracted atoms.
  23: """
  24: from __future__ import annotations
  25: 
  26: import re
  27: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  28: 
  29: from src.polaris_graph.generator.fact_dedup import (
  30:     FactSignature,
  31:     extract_signature,
  32: )
  33: 
  34: # A frozen, env-overridable default. Higher = more conservative (fewer merges).
  35: _DEFAULT_MIN_JACCARD = 0.80
  36: 
  37: # Content-token extraction: lowercase alphanumeric tokens of length >= 3, minus a
  38: # tiny closed-class stoplist. Numbers stay (they also feed the signature) so that
  39: # "20%" vs "37%" lowers Jaccard too — defense in depth with the signature check.
  40: # Hyphen/slash-preserving tokens (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): keep biomedical entity
  41: # tokens INTACT — "IL-6" / "IL-10" / "CD4/CD8" are ONE token each, not split into a
  42: # dropped "il" + a bare digit. Without this, "IL-6 ... 20%" tokenized to just {6} and
  43: # merged with "TNF ... 20%" / "IL-10 ... 20%".
  44: _TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+(?:[-/][a-z0-9]+)*")
  45: _STOPWORDS = frozenset(
  46:     "the a an and or of to in for with on at by from as is are was were be been "
  47:     "that this these those it its their his her our your they we he she than then "
  48:     "which who whom whose into over under between among within without".split()
  49: )
  50: 
  51: 
  52: def _content_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
  53:     """Tokens for the Jaccard similarity (noise-reduced: length >= 3, non-stopword)."""
  54:     toks = {
  55:         t for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
  56:         if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _STOPWORDS
  57:     }
  58:     return frozenset(toks)
  59: 
  60: 
  61: # Surrounding sentence/grouping punctuation stripped from a subject token's EDGES only;
  62: # every other character (sign +/-, slash, Greek α/β, %, internal hyphen, digits) is kept
  63: # VERBATIM so it can distinguish entities. (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P1.)
  64: _SUBJECT_EDGE_STRIP = ".,;:!?()[]{}\"'`…«»‹›—–"
  65: 
  66: 
  67: def _subject_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
  68:     """Entity/subject tokens for the SWAP guard, extracted ROBUSTLY (Codex diff-gate
  69:     iter-2/3/4 P1 class). Split on whitespace, lowercase, strip ONLY surrounding
  70:     sentence/grouping punctuation, and keep the token BODY verbatim. This preserves
  71:     EVERY clinically-distinguishing character — sign suffixes (`CD4+` vs `CD4-`, `pks+`
  72:     vs `pks-`), Greek variants (`IL-1α` vs `IL-1β`), hyphens (`IL-6` vs `IL-10`),
  73:     alphanumerics (`SGLT2` vs `DPP4`), and short symbols (`Zn` vs `Cu`) — so any two
  74:     DISTINCT entities produce DIFFERENT subject tokens and trigger the mutual-swap
  75:     block. A token with NO letter (pure number/punctuation) is excluded (numeric
  76:     differences are the signature-conflict guard's job); stopwords are dropped. Being
  77:     maximally character-preserving here only makes the dedup MORE conservative
  78:     (under-merge = §-1.1-safe; over-merge is the lethal direction we forbid)."""
  79:     out: set[str] = set()
  80:     for raw in text.lower().split():
  81:         t = raw.strip(_SUBJECT_EDGE_STRIP)
  82:         if t and t not in _STOPWORDS and any(c.isalpha() for c in t):
  83:             out.add(t)
  84:     return frozenset(out)
  85: 
  86: 
  87: def _jaccard(a: frozenset[str], b: frozenset[str]) -> float:
  88:     if not a and not b:
  89:         return 1.0
  90:     if not a or not b:
  91:         return 0.0
  92:     inter = len(a & b)
  93:     union = len(a | b)
  94:     return inter / union if union else 0.0
  95: 
  96: 
  97: def _subjects_differ(a_subjects: frozenset[str], b_subjects: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
  98:     """True iff the two claims have ANY subject-token difference (in EITHER direction).
  99: 
 100:     Codex diff-gate iter-1..5 found that a token heuristic cannot tell a non-distinguishing
 101:     elaboration ("fiber" vs "dietary fiber") from a clinically-distinct SUBGROUP narrowing
 102:     ("breast cancer" vs "HER2+ breast cancer", "T cells" vs "CD4+ T cells", "aspirin" vs
 103:     "low-dose aspirin") — both are one-sided subset modifiers. Since over-merging two
 104:     distinct clinical facts is the §-1.1 LETHAL direction and under-merging is safe, the
 105:     dedup requires IDENTICAL subject-token sets to merge: ANY subject difference (mutual
 106:     swap OR one-sided subset) blocks the merge. Only verbatim repeats / pure reorderings
 107:     (identical subjects) collapse — exactly the inflation vector the dedup must stop —
 108:     while every subject/modifier difference is preserved as a distinct claim."""
 109:     return a_subjects != b_subjects
 110: 
 111: 
 112: def _signatures_conflict(a: FactSignature, b: FactSignature) -> bool:
 113:     """True iff a populated context axis (percent-decimals OR years OR dollars) is
 114:     populated on BOTH sides AND has zero intersection — i.e. the two claims carry
 115:     DIFFERENT numbers and must NOT be merged. Mirrors
 116:     ``fact_dedup._signatures_conflict`` (the populated-axis lesson) but also guards
 117:     the decimal axis, since for claim atoms a different percentage IS a different
 118:     fact."""
 119:     if a.is_empty() or b.is_empty():
 120:         return False
 121:     decimals_conflict = (a.decimals and b.decimals) and not (a.decimals & b.decimals)
 122:     years_conflict = (a.years and b.years) and not (a.years & b.years)
 123:     dollars_conflict = (
 124:         (a.dollar_buckets and b.dollar_buckets)
 125:         and not (a.dollar_buckets & b.dollar_buckets)
 126:     )
 127:     return bool(decimals_conflict or years_conflict or dollars_conflict)
 128: 
 129: 
 130: @dataclass
 131: class DedupResult:
 132:     """Result of a dedup pass over a list of claim texts.
 133: 
 134:     ``groups`` is a list of clusters, each a list of original indices with the
 135:     REPRESENTATIVE (lowest original index) first. ``representatives`` is the list
 136:     of representative indices (one per group), in ascending order — the caller
 137:     keeps exactly these.
 138:     """
 139:     groups: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=list)
 140: 
 141:     @property
 142:     def representatives(self) -> list[int]:
 143:         return sorted(g[0] for g in self.groups)
 144: 
 145:     @property
 146:     def n_raw(self) -> int:
 147:         return sum(len(g) for g in self.groups)
 148: 
 149:     @property
 150:     def n_kept(self) -> int:
 151:         return len(self.groups)
 152: 
 153:     @property
 154:     def n_collapsed(self) -> int:
 155:         return self.n_raw - self.n_kept
 156: 
 157:     def collapsed_groups(self) -> list[list[int]]:
 158:         """Only the groups that actually merged >1 claim (for the audit trail)."""
 159:         return [g for g in self.groups if len(g) > 1]
 160: 
 161: 
 162: def dedup_claims(
 163:     texts: list[str], *, min_jaccard: float = _DEFAULT_MIN_JACCARD,
 164: ) -> DedupResult:
 165:     """Greedy deterministic claim dedup.
 166: 
 167:     Each claim joins the FIRST existing cluster it is compatible with (compatible =
 168:     no numeric-signature conflict AND no mutual subject/entity SWAP against ANY member
 169:     AND Jaccard >= min_jaccard against the cluster representative); otherwise it starts
 170:     a new cluster. Deterministic: input order is preserved, representative = lowest
 171:     index, no RNG.
 172:     """
 173:     sigs = [extract_signature(t) for t in texts]
 174:     toks = [_content_tokens(t) for t in texts]   # for Jaccard
 175:     stoks = [_subject_tokens(t) for t in texts]  # for the entity-swap guard
 176:     clusters: list[list[int]] = []
 177:     cluster_rep_tokens: list[frozenset[str]] = []
 178:     for i in range(len(texts)):
 179:         placed = False
 180:         for ci, cluster in enumerate(clusters):
 181:             # no numeric conflict AND IDENTICAL subject-token sets (any subject
 182:             # difference — swap OR one-sided subset — blocks the merge; §-1.1-safe)
 183:             if any(_signatures_conflict(sigs[i], sigs[m]) for m in cluster):
 184:                 continue
 185:             if any(_subjects_differ(stoks[i], stoks[m]) for m in cluster):
 186:                 continue
 187:             if _jaccard(toks[i], cluster_rep_tokens[ci]) >= min_jaccard:
 188:                 cluster.append(i)
 189:                 placed = True
 190:                 break
 191:         if not placed:
 192:             clusters.append([i])
 193:             cluster_rep_tokens.append(toks[i])
 194:     return DedupResult(groups=clusters)

 succeeded in 27718ms:

exec
"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "rg -n \"dedup|duplicate|claim_id|atomic|unique|reconcile|claims\" scripts\\dr_benchmark src\\polaris_graph\\benchmark tests\\dr_benchmark -g \"*.py\"" in C:\POLARIS
 succeeded in 4238ms:
   1: """Dual-§-1.1-audit reconciler (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
   2: 
   3: Combines a Claude ledger + a Codex ledger for the same (system, question) into a single
   4: reconciled ledger. **Conservative-MAX rule** (Codex PR-3 design answer C): on per-claim verdict
   5: disagreement, take the WORSE-of-two as the gating verdict — clinical-lethal framing means a
   6: problem either auditor finds counts. The disagreement is preserved in `audit_note` so the
   7: trail is transparent. Same rule for Coverage rows (worse-of-two: covered AND citation_supported
   8: both demoted on either-says-no).
   9: 
  10: Pure logic. No I/O beyond the schema dataclasses.
  11: """
  12: 
  13: from __future__ import annotations
  14: 
  15: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  16: 
  17: from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import Claim, Coverage, Ledger
  18: 
  19: # Verdict severity order (low = good, high = bad). Conservative-MAX takes the HIGHEST.
  20: _VERDICT_ORDER = {
  21:     "VERIFIED": 0,
  22:     "PARTIAL": 1,
  23:     "UNREACHABLE": 2,
  24:     "UNSUPPORTED": 3,
  25:     "FABRICATED": 4,
  26: }
  27: _SEVERITY_ORDER = {"S3": 0, "S2": 1, "S1": 2, "S0": 3}
  28: 
  29: 
  30: def _worse_verdict(a: str, b: str) -> str:
  31:     return a if _VERDICT_ORDER[a] >= _VERDICT_ORDER[b] else b
  32: 
  33: 
  34: def _worse_severity(a: str, b: str) -> str:
  35:     """When auditors disagree on severity, escalate to the HIGHER severity (more critical)."""
  36:     return a if _SEVERITY_ORDER[a] >= _SEVERITY_ORDER[b] else b
  37: 
  38: 
  39: def _carry_evidence(a: Claim, b: Claim, worse_verdict: str) -> tuple[str | None, str | None, str | None]:
  40:     """For the worse verdict, pick the evidence (span_quote / unreachable_subtype / audit_note)
  41:     from whichever auditor produced THAT verdict. If both produced the worse verdict, prefer
  42:     Claude's span/subtype (deterministic tie-break) and CONCATENATE both auditors' notes
  43:     (Codex PR-3 diff P3 #1: comment said both appended; now it actually does)."""
  44:     winners: list[Claim] = [c for c in (a, b) if c.verdict == worse_verdict]
  45:     if not winners:
  46:         winners = [a, b]  # both better than worse_verdict (impossible by construction)
  47:     primary = winners[0]
  48:     if len(winners) == 2:
  49:         # Both auditors landed on the worse verdict — preserve both notes for traceability.
  50:         notes = [c.audit_note for c in winners if c.audit_note]
  51:         merged_note = (
  52:             " || ".join(notes) if len(notes) > 1
  53:             else (notes[0] if notes else primary.audit_note)
  54:         )
  55:         return primary.span_quote, primary.unreachable_subtype, merged_note
  56:     return primary.span_quote, primary.unreachable_subtype, primary.audit_note
  57: 
  58: 
  59: def _reconcile_claim(a: Claim, b: Claim) -> Claim:
  60:     """Conservative-MAX one claim; preserve disagreement in audit_note for traceability."""
  61:     worse_v = _worse_verdict(a.verdict, b.verdict)
  62:     worse_s = _worse_severity(a.severity, b.severity)
  63:     span, subtype, note = _carry_evidence(a, b, worse_v)
  64:     disagreement = a.verdict != b.verdict or a.severity != b.severity
  65:     base_note = note or ""
  66:     if disagreement:
  67:         trail = (
  68:             f"reconciled: claude={a.verdict}/{a.severity}, codex={b.verdict}/{b.severity}; "
  69:             f"conservative-MAX -> {worse_v}/{worse_s}"
  70:         )
  71:         full_note = f"{base_note} | {trail}" if base_note else trail
  72:     else:
  73:         full_note = note
  74:     # citation_id: prefer non-null; if both non-null but differ, prefer the worse_v auditor's.
  75:     citation = a.citation_id if (a.verdict == worse_v and a.citation_id) else (
  76:         b.citation_id if b.citation_id else a.citation_id
  77:     )
  78:     return Claim(
  79:         claim_id=a.claim_id,
  80:         severity=worse_s,
  81:         verdict=worse_v,
  82:         citation_id=citation,
  83:         span_quote=span,
  84:         unreachable_subtype=subtype if worse_v == "UNREACHABLE" else None,
  85:         audit_note=full_note,
  86:     )
  87: 
  88: 
  89: def _reconcile_coverage(a: Coverage, b: Coverage) -> Coverage:
  90:     """Conservative-MAX coverage: covered AND citation_supported each fall to the WORSE of two
  91:     (either auditor says NO -> NO). Disagreement preserved in auditor_note."""
  92:     covered = a.covered and b.covered
  93:     cs = a.citation_supported and b.citation_supported
  94:     if a.covered != b.covered or a.citation_supported != b.citation_supported:
  95:         trail = (
  96:             f"reconciled: claude=covered={a.covered}/cited={a.citation_supported}, "
  97:             f"codex=covered={b.covered}/cited={b.citation_supported}; "
  98:             f"conservative-MAX -> covered={covered}/cited={cs}"
  99:         )
 100:         base = a.auditor_note or b.auditor_note or ""
 101:         note = f"{base} | {trail}" if base else trail
 102:     else:
 103:         note = a.auditor_note or b.auditor_note
 104:     return Coverage(
 105:         element_id=a.element_id,
 106:         covered=covered,
 107:         citation_supported=cs,
 108:         auditor_note=note,
 109:     )
 110: 
 111: 
 112: def reconcile(claude: Ledger, codex: Ledger) -> Ledger:
 113:     """Reconcile two single-auditor ledgers for the same (system, question) into one.
 114: 
 115:     Raises ValueError if the ledgers disagree on identity (system/question/rubric_sha256) —
 116:     that is a §-1.1 pre-registration violation, not a reconciliation case."""
 117:     if claude.system != codex.system:
 118:         raise ValueError(f"system mismatch: claude={claude.system}, codex={codex.system}")
 119:     if claude.question_id != codex.question_id:
 120:         raise ValueError(
 121:             f"question_id mismatch: claude={claude.question_id}, codex={codex.question_id}"
 122:         )
 123:     if claude.rubric_sha256 != codex.rubric_sha256:
 124:         raise ValueError(
 125:             "rubric_sha256 mismatch — auditors audited against DIFFERENT pinned rubrics: "
 126:             f"claude={claude.rubric_sha256}, codex={codex.rubric_sha256}"
 127:         )
 128:     if claude.auditor != "claude" or codex.auditor != "codex":
 129:         raise ValueError("reconcile(claude, codex) requires correctly-tagged auditors")
 130: 
 131:     # Join claims by claim_id (require both auditors to cover each claim).
 132:     claude_claims = {c.claim_id: c for c in claude.claims}
 133:     codex_claims = {c.claim_id: c for c in codex.claims}
 134:     all_claim_ids = sorted(set(claude_claims) | set(codex_claims))
 135:     reconciled_claims: list[Claim] = []
 136:     for cid in all_claim_ids:
 137:         a = claude_claims.get(cid)
 138:         b = codex_claims.get(cid)
 139:         if a is None or b is None:
 140:             # Either auditor missed this claim: the missing auditor's silence is treated as
 141:             # WORSE-than-VERIFIED (UNSUPPORTED+audit_note) — conservative discipline. The other
 142:             # auditor's row carries forward, escalated.
 143:             #
 144:             # Codex PR-3 diff P1 #2: when the present row is UNREACHABLE, we cannot just
 145:             # escalate its verdict to UNSUPPORTED while keeping `unreachable_subtype` — Claim
 146:             # validation forbids subtype except on UNREACHABLE. Two cases:
 147:             #   - present.verdict == UNREACHABLE: keep UNREACHABLE (worse than VERIFIED;
 148:             #     escalation goal already met) + keep subtype.
 149:             #   - present.verdict != UNREACHABLE: escalate to UNSUPPORTED, drop any subtype,
 150:             #     and ensure traceability via audit_note (UNSUPPORTED+cited requires it).
 151:             present = a or b
 152:             # Codex PR-3 diff iter2 P3: silent_side names the auditor that was SILENT —
 153:             # if claude (a) is present, codex was silent (and vice versa). Earlier
 154:             # assignment was reversed (self-contradictory text; scoring unaffected).
 155:             present_side = "claude" if a is not None else "codex"
 156:             silent_side = "codex" if a is not None else "claude"
 157:             base_note = present.audit_note or ""
 158:             escalation_note = (
 159:                 f"reconciled: only one auditor produced a row "
 160:                 f"({present_side}); {silent_side} silent -> escalated"
 161:             )
 162:             full_note = f"{base_note} | {escalation_note}" if base_note else escalation_note
 163:             if present.verdict == "UNREACHABLE":
 164:                 # Already worse than VERIFIED; keep UNREACHABLE + subtype.
 165:                 reconciled_claims.append(Claim(
 166:                     claim_id=cid,
 167:                     severity=present.severity,
 168:                     verdict="UNREACHABLE",
 169:                     citation_id=present.citation_id,
 170:                     span_quote=present.span_quote,
 171:                     unreachable_subtype=present.unreachable_subtype,
 172:                     audit_note=full_note,
 173:                 ))
 174:             else:
 175:                 escalated_verdict = _worse_verdict(present.verdict, "UNSUPPORTED")
 176:                 # If we land on FABRICATED/PARTIAL we need a span_quote; if the present row
 177:                 # has none, keep verdict=UNSUPPORTED instead (no span fabrication).
 178:                 if escalated_verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not present.span_quote:
 179:                     escalated_verdict = "UNSUPPORTED"
 180:                 reconciled_claims.append(Claim(
 181:                     claim_id=cid,
 182:                     severity=present.severity,
 183:                     verdict=escalated_verdict,
 184:                     citation_id=present.citation_id,
 185:                     span_quote=present.span_quote,
 186:                     unreachable_subtype=None,  # not UNREACHABLE -> must be None
 187:                     audit_note=full_note,
 188:                 ))
 189:         else:
 190:             reconciled_claims.append(_reconcile_claim(a, b))
 191: 
 192:     # Join coverage by element_id (same discipline).
 193:     claude_cov = {c.element_id: c for c in claude.coverage}
 194:     codex_cov = {c.element_id: c for c in codex.coverage}
 195:     all_element_ids = sorted(set(claude_cov) | set(codex_cov))
 196:     reconciled_coverage: list[Coverage] = []
 197:     for eid in all_element_ids:
 198:         a = claude_cov.get(eid)
 199:         b = codex_cov.get(eid)
 200:         if a is None or b is None:
 201:             present = a or b
 202:             reconciled_coverage.append(Coverage(
 203:                 element_id=eid,
 204:                 covered=False,  # missing-auditor escalates to NOT covered (conservative)
 205:                 citation_supported=False,
 206:                 auditor_note=(
 207:                     f"reconciled: only one auditor ({'claude' if a else 'codex'}) covered this element"
 208:                 ),
 209:             ))
 210:         else:
 211:             reconciled_coverage.append(_reconcile_coverage(a, b))
 212: 
 213:     return Ledger(
 214:         system=claude.system,
 215:         question_id=claude.question_id,
 216:         auditor="reconciled",
 217:         audit_method=f"reconciled-from:claude+codex (conservative-MAX); "
 218:                      f"upstream: {claude.audit_method} / {codex.audit_method}",
 219:         audit_timestamp_utc=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
 220:         rubric_sha256=claude.rubric_sha256,
 221:         claims=reconciled_claims,
 222:         coverage=reconciled_coverage,
 223:     )

 succeeded in 14695ms:

codex
`ClaimRow` and `ledger_schema.Claim` truly have no report-text field, and schema validation enforces unique `claim_id`s. One caveat I’m weighing is that `claim_id` uniqueness is not semantic dedup by itself; I’m checking whether this creates an execution blocker for this wiring diff or just a methodological caveat outside the live call site.
